posts.description:
Severe tooth pain does not wait for a convenient time. Whether you wake up with a throbbing molar, notice swelling along your gumline, or crack a tooth on a weekday afternoon, the question is usually the same: do I need a root canal or a tooth extraction?

Both treatments can relieve pain. But they solve different problems, and the right choice depends on the condition of your tooth, not just how much it hurts.

This guide is written for patients in Midtown Manhattan and Tribeca, NYC who are dealing with urgent dental pain and want to understand their options before walking into an emergency dentist’s office. If you are already in pain, Expert Dental NYC offers same-day urgent dental care at both locations.

Root Canal vs Tooth Extraction: What Is the Main Difference?
The simplest way to explain the difference: a root canal saves the tooth, and an extraction removes it.

A root canal removes the infected or damaged tissue inside the tooth, cleans the inner canal system, and seals it so the tooth can remain in your mouth. The outer shell of the tooth stays intact.

A tooth extraction removes the entire tooth when it is too damaged, fractured, or infected to function reliably, even after treatment.

Both procedures address tooth infection and pain. The deciding factor is whether the tooth can be predictably restored and used long-term.

In emergency dental care in NYC, dentists evaluate the tooth with X-rays and a clinical exam before recommending either option. Tooth infection

If you are looking into dental implants in NYC, you may have heard the term “guided implant surgery” and wondered what it means and whether it applies to your case.

Guided dental implant surgery uses 3D imaging, digital treatment planning software, and a custom surgical guide to help position your implant with greater accuracy than freehand placement. The dentist maps your jawbone, nerves, and anatomy in detail before you ever sit in the surgical chair. That data then drives the design of a physical guide that controls exactly where, at what angle, and how deep the implant goes.

This article is specifically about how that digital-to-physical planning process works and why it matters for implant outcomes. It is not a repeat of what a CBCT scan does. The scan is the input. Guided surgery is what happens with that data.

At Expert Dental NYC, patients in Midtown Manhattan and Tribeca can access this technology as part of a fully planned implant workflow, supported by experienced implant specialists in-house.

What Is Guided Dental Implant Surgery?
Guided implant surgery is a method of placing dental implants using a pre-designed digital plan and a physical guide that carries that plan into the mouth during the procedure.

Before the day of surgery, the dentist uses digital scans and implant planning software to map out exactly where the implant should go. The position, angulation, and depth are determined in advance, not in the moment. A custom surgical guide is then created b

Many patients assume that dental implant success depends only on the implant itself. In reality, the strength and health of the jawbone play a major role in whether treatment succeeds long term. Bone quality and density directly affect how well an implant can anchor, heal, and remain stable after placement.

Patients in Midtown Manhattan and Tribeca NYC who are considering dental implants in NYC often hear terms like “bone loss” or “low bone density” during their consultation, but many do not fully understand what those terms mean. Bone density, bone volume, and overall jawbone health all influence implant stability, long-term support, and how well an implant may perform over time.

This guide explains what bone quality for dental implants means in implant dentistry, how it affects the dental implant success rate, and what treatment options may help if bone support is limited. If you are planning treatment with an implant dentist in NYC, understanding your bone health is an important part of choosing the right approach.

What Is Bone Quality and Why It Matters for Dental Implants
Bone quality refers to the strength, density, structure, and volume of the jawbone where an implant will be placed. When dentists evaluate bone quality for dental implants, they do not simply check whether bone is present. They assess whether the bone is strong enough, thick enough, and healthy enough to support long-term implant success.

Dental implants function differently than bridges or dentures

When a tooth becomes damaged from decay, cracks, fractures, or everyday wear, many patients are surprised to learn there may be several ways to repair it. A dentist may recommend dental inlays and onlays, a dental crown, or another restorative treatment depending on how much of the tooth has been affected. For many patients in Midtown Manhattan and Tribeca, the challenge is understanding the difference between these restorations and knowing why one option may be better than another.

This guide explains what dental inlays, onlays, and crowns are, when each treatment is recommended, and how they compare in strength, cost, durability, and tooth preservation. If you have been told you may need an onlay or crown treatment, or are trying to understand a dental inlay vs crown recommendation, this article will help you make a more informed decision about your treatment options.

What Are Dental Inlays and Onlays and How Do They Work?
Dentists often think of restorative treatments as part of a spectrum based on how much of the tooth needs repair. A small cavity may only need a filling, while a severely damaged tooth may require a full dental crown. Dental inlays and onlays fall in the middle of that spectrum.

In simple terms:

A filling repairs small areas of damage
An inlay repairs moderate damage inside the tooth
An onlay repairs larger damage involving the chewing surface and cusps
A crown covers the entire visible tooth
Understanding what dental inlays and onlays are helps patie

Dental implant success depends on accurate planning before any procedure begins, not just surgical skill. A CBCT scan, also known as cone beam computed tomography, is a modern 3D dental imaging tool that uses advanced imaging technology and a cone-shaped x-ray beam to allow dentists to evaluate the jaw, bone, and surrounding dental structures in detail. This type of diagnostic imaging plays a key role in dental implant planning by helping identify the safest and most effective placement of dental implants for each patient.

For patients in Midtown Manhattan and Tribeca, CBCT technology improves precision, safety, and predictability during implant treatment. It allows the dental team and other dental professionals to assess bone quality, locate important structures, and plan implant placement with confidence. This article explains why CBCT scans are often recommended before dental implants and how this imaging supports better long-term results and the overall success of your implant.

What Is a CBCT Scan and Why Is It Used in Dental Implant Planning?
A CBCT scan, or cone beam computed tomography scan, is a type of 3D dental imaging that gives your dentist a much clearer view of your teeth, jaw, and bone than traditional dental x-rays. A CBCT scan dental system creates panoramic, cross-sectional, and detailed three-dimensional images, which helps the dental team see the exact area where a dental implant may be placed. This matters because the planning of implant treatment depen

Same-day dental implants, also called immediate-load dental implants, let a dentist place the implant and attach a temporary dental crown in one visit. For some patients, this can offer teeth on the same day replacement instead of waiting through multiple appointments. Still, same-day treatment is not right for everyone. A full consultation is needed to check bone support, gum health, and other factors before a treatment plan is recommended.

For patients with missing teeth in Midtown Manhattan and Tribeca, this guide explains what to expect and what may affect eligibility. At Expert Dental NYC, a top-rated dental provider known across New York’s Manhattan dental community, advanced technology and surgical expertise help support careful planning for dental implants in Manhattan. This article is designed to help curious or motivated patients understand how same-day care works and whether it may be the right next step.

What Are Same Day Dental Implants and How Do They Work?
Same-day dental implants are a teeth replacement option that allows a dentist to place the implant post and attach a temporary crown in one visit. You may also hear this called immediate-load dental implants, single-day dental implants, or teeth in one day. Compared to traditional multi-stage treatment, this approach lets some patients leave the dental office with a visible tooth the same day instead of waiting months between steps.

In this implant procedure, the dental implant is placed into the jawbone d

Replacing a missing tooth often comes down to two common solutions: dental implants and dental bridges. Both treatments restore function and appearance, but they work in different ways. A dental implant replaces a missing tooth root with a titanium post placed in the jawbone and topped with an implant-supported crown. A dental bridge fills the gap by anchoring an artificial tooth to neighboring teeth or implants.

For patients seeking tooth replacement in NYC, both options are widely used in modern restorative dentistry. The right choice often depends on bone health, treatment timeline, long-term durability, cost, and aesthetic goals. Patients visiting a cosmetic dentistry practice in Manhattan, including those near Tribeca or Midtown Manhattan, are typically evaluated for both options during consultation.

This guide compares dental implants vs dental bridges, explains their benefits and limitations, and helps NYC patients understand how dentists determine which option may be the best fit.

What Are Dental Implants and How Do They Work?
Dental implants are artificial tooth roots that replace missing teeth by integrating with the jawbone. A titanium dental implant is surgically placed into the jaw where a tooth root once existed. Over several months, the implant fuses with the bone through a biological process known as osseointegration. This integration creates a stable foundation for a replacement tooth.

Once healing is complete, the dentist attaches a connector called an a

Dental implants are designed to replace missing teeth with strength and stability. When properly maintained, a dental implant can last for many years. However, the gums and bone around dental implants can become inflamed or infected. This condition is called peri-implantitis. It is a type of peri-implant disease that affects the tissues surrounding a dental implant, not the implant itself.

Peri-implantitis can develop months or even years after implant placement if daily care and professional monitoring are not consistent. Over time, untreated inflammation can lead to bone loss around the implant and increase the risk of implant failure.

Long-term implant success depends on a shared commitment between you and your dental team. For patients in Midtown Manhattan, staying proactive with dental implant maintenance helps protect both your oral health and your investment. This guide explains how peri-implantitis develops and how to prevent it through proper home care and ongoing professional support.

What Is Peri-Implantitis and Why Does It Threaten Dental Implants in NYC
Peri-implantitis is one of several peri-implant diseases and conditions that affect the tissues around dental implants in NYC. It is a biological complication, not a flaw in the implant itself. Dental implants do not get cavities. The concern is infection and inflammation in the gums and bone around the implant.

When bacteria collect around dental implants, the surrounding soft tissue can become inflamed. This

Mini dental implants in NYC are smaller versions of traditional dental implants that can replace missing teeth or help stabilize a denture. They use a narrow titanium screw that sits in the jawbone and supports a replacement tooth or denture attachment. Dentists often recommend them when space is tight or when the jawbone cannot support a full-sized implant.

If you are looking into tooth replacement in Midtown or searching for an implant dentist in Tribeca, you may have questions about whether mini implants fit your needs. Many New York dental patients explore mini implants after hearing they do not have enough jawbone density for traditional dental implants or that they may need a bone graft. Mini implants can offer a less invasive option in certain cases, especially for patients dealing with bone loss or denture instability.

This guide explains how mini dental implants in NYC differ from full-size dental implants, who they tend to work best for, and what tradeoffs to understand. It is written for patients comparing treatment options at a dental implant center in Midtown Manhattan or Tribeca, NYC, including people with smaller jawbones, mild bone loss, or dentures that do not stay firmly in place.

Schedule Online Today
What Are Mini Dental Implants?
Mini dental implants are a type of dental implant designed for situations where traditional dental implants may not work. They can replace missing teeth or help secure a denture using small-diameter implants that fit in tighte

Digital Smile Design combines advanced imaging, smile design software, and careful facial analysis to create results that are personal, precise, and predictable.

In Midtown Manhattan and Tribeca, patients often expect a high level of accuracy and control when it comes to cosmetic treatments, especially in digital dentistry. Digital Smile Design meets that expectation with the help of advanced digital tools. It allows a cosmetic dentist in Manhattan to plan veneers, bonding, whitening, and full smile makeovers around the patient’s face, not just their teeth, through advanced aesthetic dentistry techniques. For many NYC patients, the ability to approve a smile design in advance builds trust and confidence from the start.

This guide explains how digital smile design in NYC works, who benefits from it most, and why patients looking for veneers in NYC, a smile makeover in NYC, or other aesthetic treatments often choose practices that offer this level of planning and visualization.

What Is Digital Smile Design and Why It’s Revolutionizing NYC Cosmetic Dentistry?
Digital Smile Design uses digital tools such as high-resolution photos, facial videos, and 3D scans to design a smile that fits the patient’s facial features, personality, and goals. Rather than relying only on dental measurements, the process looks at how the teeth relate to the lips, gums, and face during speech and smiling.

For patients, digital smile design (DSD) in NYC feels more transparent than traditional cosmet

summary.snippet: "Super friendly staff from the reception, hygienist, to the dentist."
"Amazing service and quality of place is well put!"
"Just had a tooth cleaning and filling done!"

most_relevant.description:
Normally, I am not the kind to write reviews but this one I really had to point it out. I went for a oral surgery consultation regarding my wisdom tooth that was sitting close to my nerves. Dr. Rothenburg diagnosed and all was going smoothly before I got to discuss the finances.

$13K out of pocket for two wisdom teeth removal was asked which is crazy and unheard of. Then I researched a bit more on the procedures that I was being charged for and asked some dentist friends and found out half the procedures like the PRP, Bone replacement is not necessary for third molar extraction and this was worth $4K just by itself.

Additionally I was quoted $540 for Nitrous Oxide (Laughing Gas) and $2500 for sedation which is way over the market price.

When asked about my insurance on the phone before making the appointment, they had let me know they were in service providers and hence had negotiated adjusted rates. During the finances, they told me only $300 for extraction is covered by my insurance which also left me baffled. I called my insurance and found out most if not all other procedures were covered at 50% upto $1000 which the financial office did not even consider. I do not know why and hence decided for a second opinion by another oral surgeon.

Just got out of my surgery from another oral surgeon in UWS and got it all done for $1K for two teeth. From $13K down to $1K haha. All I want to say is stay safe people and research well, people may very well fell for these financial traps and pay so much more than they should.

Expert Dental NYC - please make sure to clear later on first if the insurance covers it, what are needed/ recommended / not needed and considerate of people' hard earned money.

Thank you!

I had tooth extraction on February 4 2026 by Dr Rita that performed excellent job, talked to me step by step professionally and friendly way. She extracted the tooth l did not even had much pain. Thanks Dr Rita. Ms . Leslie did xray for me and she was so nice and professional. My thanks to Ms Sophia and Amy. I recommend this Dental office to anyone who needs to be comfortable with Dental work, they will make you feel comfortable.. The office is extremely clean and neat. Thanks to everyone

I hadn’t had a professional cleaning in years and was honestly a bit nervous, but I had a fantastic experience here! The cleaning was completely painless, which was a huge relief. They are incredibly efficient—I was in and out of the office in no time. I’m also starting my Invisalign journey with Dr. Lin, the orthodontist. He’s great and made the whole process easy to understand. Highly recommend! Don’t forget Rubina who’s super friendly and the math wizard!

had an exceptional experience with the customer service at this Midtown dental office. From the moment I walked in, the staff was warm, welcoming, and highly attentive. The front desk team handled check-in efficiently and made sure all my questions were answered clearly.
What stood out most was the level of professionalism and care shown throughout my visit. The dental team took the time to explain each step of the process, ensuring I felt comfortable and informed. Their patience and genuine concern for my well-being made a significant difference. I would highly recommend Expert Dental to anyone who seeking expert dental care.

Everyone in the office is very nice and understanding. They were able to work with my less than ideal HMO plan and get me a crown that I needed.

Dr. Kwok did my crown work and was so patient. Dr. Charms saw me for a filling that had broken off and he was able to refer me to Dr. Kwok for prompt work despite his busy schedule that day. Dr. Gao helped me plan my treatment going forward and she was
clear and patient in explaining everything. Rubina the treatment coordinator is a life saver!

Kudos to everyone at Expert Dental on a job well done! I had a front upper dental bridge installed last week after losing a tooth. I could not afford an implant but was advised by Dr Khezri a more affordable option for me was the bridge. I want to give a huge shoutout to all involved for helping me smile again with confidence at almost 68 years old! Special thanks to Dr Khezri and assistant Maria as well as everyone else at the front desk. One last special thank you to Zaida who helped me along these past few trying months with so much support and kindness to this old Jersey City guy! Thank you all from the bottom of my heart, your help and kindness will never be forgotten.
Sincerely and with respect…
James(Jim) Houzner

Love this place, been coming for years. Even when I moved out of the city I commute here from Jersey because Dr. Charms is the best! Also the clinic’s whole staff is amazing and friendly. Highly recommend!!

I’ve been going here for a long time and the staff is great. Super personable and friendly. Patient relations, Robina (I apologize) is AWESOME! I wish every medical office had someone as thorough, respectful, funny, and informative as she is. I miss Kayla, but overall, definitely 10/10
would recommend.
